speaker: PERICLES
text: Yet cease your ire, you angry stars of heaven!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Wind, rain, and thunder, remember earthly man

speaker: PERICLES
text: Is but a substance that must yield to you,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And I, as fits my nature, do obey you.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Alas, the seas hath cast me on the rocks,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Washed me from shore to shore, and left my breath

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nothing to think on but ensuing death.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Let it suffice the greatness of your powers

speaker: PERICLES
text: To have bereft a prince of all his fortunes;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And, having thrown him from your wat’ry grave,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Here to have death in peace is all he’ll crave.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: What ho, Pilch!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Ha, come and bring away the nets!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: What, Patchbreech, I say!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: What say you, master?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Look how thou stirr’st now! Come

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: away, or I’ll fetch thee with a wanion.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Faith, master, I am thinking of the

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: poor men that were cast away before us even now.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Alas, poor souls, it grieved my heart

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: to hear what pitiful cries they made to us to help

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: them, when, welladay, we could scarce help

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: ourselves!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Nay, master, said not I as much

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: when I saw the porpoise how he bounced and tumbled?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: They say they’re half fish, half flesh. A plague

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: on them! They ne’er come but I look to be washed.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Master, I marvel how the fishes live in the sea.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Why, as men do a-land: the great

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: ones eat up the little ones. I can compare our rich

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: misers to nothing so fitly as to a whale: he plays

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: and tumbles, driving the poor fry before him and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: at last devours them all at a mouthful. Such

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: whales have I heard on a’ the land, who never leave

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: gaping till they swallowed the whole parish—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: church, steeple, bells and all.

speaker: PERICLES
text: A pretty moral.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: But, master, if I had been the sexton,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: I would have been that day in the belfry.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Why, man?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Because he should have swallowed

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: me too. And when I had been in his belly, I would

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: have kept such a jangling of the bells that he should

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: never have left till he cast bells, steeple, church, and

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: parish up again. But if the good King Simonides

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: were of my mind—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Simonides?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: We would purge the land of these

speaker:
THIRD
               
              FISHERMAN
text: drones that rob the bee of her honey.

speaker: PERICLES
text: How from the finny subject of the sea

speaker: PERICLES
text: These fishers tell the infirmities of men,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And from their wat’ry empire recollect

speaker: PERICLES
text: All that may men approve or men detect!—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Peace be at your labor, honest fishermen.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Honest good fellow, what’s that? If

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: it be a day fits you, search out of the calendar, and

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: nobody look after it!

speaker: PERICLES
text: May see the sea hath cast upon your coast—

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: What a drunken knave was the sea

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: to cast thee in our way!

speaker: PERICLES
text: A man whom both the waters and the wind

speaker: PERICLES
text: In that vast tennis court hath made the ball

speaker: PERICLES
text: For them to play upon entreats you pity him.

speaker: PERICLES
text: He asks of you that never used to beg.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: No, friend, cannot you beg? Here’s

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: them in our country of Greece gets more with begging

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: than we can do with working.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Canst thou catch any

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: fishes, then?

speaker: PERICLES
text: I never practiced it.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Nay, then, thou wilt starve sure,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: for here’s nothing to be got nowadays unless thou

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: canst fish for ’t.

speaker: PERICLES
text: What I have been I have forgot to know,

speaker: PERICLES
text: But what I am want teaches me to think on:

speaker: PERICLES
text: A man thronged up with cold. My veins are chill

speaker: PERICLES
text: And have no more of life than may suffice

speaker: PERICLES
text: To give my tongue that heat to ask your help—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which, if you shall refuse, when I am dead,

speaker: PERICLES
text: For that I am a man, pray you see me buried.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Die, quotha? Now gods forbid ’t, an I

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: have a gown. Here, come, put it on; keep thee

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text:
warm.
                 
                Pericles
                 
                puts
                 
                on
                 
                the
                 
                garment
                .
               Now, afore

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: me, a handsome fellow! Come, thou shalt go home,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: and we’ll have flesh for holidays, fish for fasting

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: days, and, moreo’er, puddings and flapjacks, and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: thou shalt be welcome.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thank you, sir.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Hark you, my friend. You said you

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: could not beg?

speaker: PERICLES
text: I did but crave.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: But crave? Then I’ll turn craver

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: too, and so I shall ’scape whipping.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Why, are your beggars whipped, then?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: O, not all, my friend, not all; for if

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: all your beggars were whipped, I would wish no

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: better office than to be beadle.—But, master, I’ll go

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: draw up the net.

speaker: PERICLES
text: How well this honest mirth becomes their labor!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Hark you, sir, do you know where

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: you are?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Not well.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Why, I’ll tell you. This is called Pentapolis,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: and our king the good Simonides.

speaker: PERICLES
text: “The good Simonides” do you call him?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Ay, sir, and he deserves so to be called

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: for his peaceable reign and good government.

speaker: PERICLES
text: He is a happy king, since he gains from his

speaker: PERICLES
text: subjects the name of “good” by his government.

speaker: PERICLES
text: How far is his court distant from this shore?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Marry, sir, half a day’s journey. And

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: I’ll tell you, he hath a fair daughter, and tomorrow

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: is her birthday; and there are princes and knights

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: come from all parts of the world to joust and tourney

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: for her love.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Were my fortunes equal to my desires, I

speaker: PERICLES
text: could wish to make one there.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: O, sir, things must be as they may;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: and what a man cannot get he may lawfully deal

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: for his wife’s soul.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Help, master, help! Here’s a fish

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: hangs in the net like a poor man’s right in the law:

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: ’twill hardly come out. Ha! Bots on ’t, ’tis come at

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: last, and ’tis turned to a rusty armor.

speaker: PERICLES
text: An armor, friends? I pray you let me see it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thanks, Fortune, yet, that after all thy crosses

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thou givest me somewhat to repair myself;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And though it was mine own, part of my heritage

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which my dead father did bequeath to me

speaker: PERICLES
text: With this strict charge even as he left his life,

speaker: PERICLES
text: “Keep it, my Pericles; it hath been a shield

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Twixt me and death,” and pointed to this brace,

speaker: PERICLES
text: “For that it saved me, keep it. In like necessity—

speaker: PERICLES
text: The which the gods protect thee from—may ’t defend thee.”

speaker: PERICLES
text: It kept where I kept, I so dearly loved it,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Till the rough seas, that spares not any man,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Took it in rage, though calmed have given ’t again.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thank thee for ’t; my shipwrack now’s no ill

speaker: PERICLES
text: Since I have here my father gave in his will.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: What mean you, sir?

speaker: PERICLES
text: To beg of you, kind friends, this coat of worth,

speaker: PERICLES
text: For it was sometime target to a king;

speaker: PERICLES
text: I know it by this mark. He loved me dearly,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And for his sake I wish the having of it,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And that you’d guide me to your sovereign’s court,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where with it I may appear a gentleman.

speaker: PERICLES
text: And if that ever my low fortune’s better,

speaker: PERICLES
text: I’ll pay your bounties; till then, rest your debtor.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Why, wilt thou tourney for the lady?

speaker: PERICLES
text: I’ll show the virtue I have borne in arms.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Why, do ’ee take it, and the gods give

speaker:
FIRST
               
              FISHERMAN
text: thee good on ’t.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: Ay, but hark you, my friend, ’twas

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: we that made up this garment through the rough

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: seams of the waters. There are certain condolements,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: certain vails. I hope, sir, if you thrive, you’ll

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: remember from whence you had them.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Believe ’t, I will.

speaker: PERICLES
text: By your furtherance I am clothed in steel,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And spite of all the rupture of the sea,

speaker: PERICLES
text: This jewel holds his biding on my arm.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Unto thy value I will mount myself

speaker: PERICLES
text: Upon a courser, whose delightful steps

speaker: PERICLES
text: Shall make the gazer joy to see him tread.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Only, my friend, I yet am unprovided

speaker: PERICLES
text: Of a pair of bases.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: We’ll sure provide. Thou shalt have

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: my best gown to make thee a pair; and I’ll bring

speaker:
SECOND
               
              FISHERMAN
text: thee to the court myself.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then honor be but a goal to my will;

speaker: PERICLES
text: This day I’ll rise or else add ill to ill.
